page: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features dedicated compute resources powered by Intel Xeon 8280L processors with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 104 physical cores and 208 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz, supported by hyper-threading. It is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 11400.0 GB, translating to 54.81 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 8389 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs integrated into this server type.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 25%. However, the massive core count drives top-tier multi-core performance, placing stress-ng multi-core CPU, passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression in the top 10% of benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 10444.18 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 305.41 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, executing 159.37 million operations per second.

This server type represents a high-density resource profile optimized for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw single-threaded speed. The qualitative cost efficiency is centered on its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for consolidating large-scale enterprise databases and in-memory analytics. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core performance, which may limit efficiency for single-threaded tasks. Appropriate workloads include large-scale in-memory databases like SAP HANA, massive Redis deployments, heavy database operations, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the MSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8280L processors with 104 physical cores, 208 vCPUs, and a 2.7 GHz clock speed. The server features an extensive 11400.0 GB of RAM, providing 54.81 GB per core, alongside 8389 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. While single-core benchmarks like stress-ng and single-threaded compression are weak, its multi-core and memory bandwidth performance rank in the top 10% of benchmarks. This includes top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng, passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations at 159.37 million operations per second. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale in-memory databases, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 208 vCPUs, 11400 GB of RAM, and 8389 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M416-208ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 208 vCPUs, 11.4 TB RAM, and 8.3 TB local storage for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive RAM capacity and top-tier multi-core throughput for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8280L processors with 104 physical cores and 208 vCPUs
Massive 11400.0 GB memory capacity offering 54.81 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 8389 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 10%
Excellent fit for large-scale in-memory databases and Redis deployments
